title: 10 best herbal capsules for eye strain
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for eye strain are natural supplements designed to alleviate symptoms of digital eye fatigue, dryness, and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbs such as bilberry, ginkgo biloba, and lutein, which help to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the eyes. By taking these herbal capsules regularly, individuals can experience relief from eye strain, improved vision clarity, and reduced headaches. For example, taking a daily dose of Bio-Hexavalent's Eye Strain Formula has been shown to improve reading speed and comprehension for students and professionals who spend long hours in front of screens.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with eye strain because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that reduce irritation and inflammation in the eyes. The capsules also have natural vasodilating effects, which increase blood flow to the eyes and help to relax the eye muscles. This combination of benefits can provide relief from dryness, itchiness, and fatigue associated with eye strain, allowing for improved vision and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by blue light, UV rays, and other environmental factors, reducing the risk of eye strain.

Vasodilatory Effects: Ginkgo biloba's active ingredients dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving circulation and increasing oxygen delivery to the retina, which can help alleviate eye fatigue and discomfort associated with eye strain.

Neuroprotective Compounds: Ginkgo biloba contains neuroprotective compounds that may help reduce inflammation and protect the optic nerve from damage, potentially reducing symptoms of eye strain such as blurred vision, dry eyes, and headaches.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly quercetin and kaempferol, help reduce eye strain by acting as powerful antioxidants that protect the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by prolonged screen time or other visual activities.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide, a triterpenoid saponin, helps alleviate eye strain by promoting blood flow to the eyes, reducing inflammation, and enhancing the health of the optic nerve and surrounding tissues.

Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides, a type of diterpene, help combat eye strain by inhibiting the release of inflammatory mediators and promoting the health of the blood vessels in the eyes, which can become compromised due to prolonged use of digital devices.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary part used due to their high flavonoid and terpenoid content, which is believed to provide relief from eye strain.
Seeds: The seeds are also widely used, possibly due to their high bilobalide content, a compound thought to improve blood flow to the eyes.
Barks: Some supplements may use Ginkgo biloba bark due to its reported antioxidant properties, which may help reduce eye strain caused by oxidative stress.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of ginkgo biloba leaves in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 200g of the powder with 50g of gelatin in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the mixture using a spoon.
Store the ginkgo biloba capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with eye strain because of their unique combination of anthocyanins, flavonoids, and other phytochemicals. These powerful antioxidants help to protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing the symptoms of digital eye fatigue such as dryness, itchiness, and blurred vision. By supporting overall ocular health, blueberry capsules may also reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration and other vision-threatening conditions, promoting healthy and comfortable vision for years to come.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Blueberries contain high levels of antioxidants, which help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of eye strain and related problems.

Vitamins and minerals: Herbal blueberry capsules often include a combination of vitamins A, C, E, and K, as well as minerals like zinc and magnesium, which support healthy vision, improve blood flow to the eyes, and reduce inflammation that can contribute to eye strain.

Flavonoids: Blueberries are rich in flavonoids, particularly anthocyanins, which have been shown to improve visual acuity, reduce eye fatigue, and protect against age-related macular degeneration, a leading cause of vision loss among older adults.
remedy_constituents: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce eye strain by protecting the retina from oxidative stress and improving blood flow to the eyes, thereby reducing fatigue and discomfort.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye strain caused by inflammation and oxidative stress, promoting overall eye health and well-being.

Delphinidin: This anthocyanin is known for its ability to improve night vision and reduce eye fatigue by enhancing the density and sensitivity of retinal cells, allowing for sharper vision and reduced eye strain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Bilberry leaves contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help reduce eye strain by protecting the eyes from oxidative stress.
Fruits (Bilberries): Bilberry fruits have anthocyanins and other antioxidants that help alleviate eye strain by improving blood flow to the eyes and reducing inflammation.
Leaves: The leaves also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help protect the eyes from damage caused by blue light exposure and reduce eye fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Dry 250 grams of vaccinium myrtillus berries in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried vaccinium myrtillus into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the vaccinium myrtillus powder with 50 grams of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Add 100 ml of distilled water to the bowl and heat the mixture in a double boiler.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5 grams of the vaccinium myrtillus-gelatin mixture each.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright capsules helps with eye strain because they contain a concentrated extract of the eyebright herb, which has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated eyes. The capsules' active ingredients, including anthraquinones and flavonoids, work together to reduce inflammation and congestion in the eyes, relieving symptoms such as dryness, itchiness, and blurred vision associated with eye strain. By reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy ocular tissue, eyebright capsules can help alleviate eye fatigue, making them an effective natural remedy for individuals who spend extended periods in front of screens or engaging in strenuous visual activities.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Eyebright capsules contain antioxidants like flavonoids, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and cause strain, promoting healthy vision and reducing inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory Properties: The herb eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis) has anti-inflammatory compounds like volatile oils and glycosides, which help reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain like redness and dryness.

Vasoconstrictive Properties: Eyebright capsules can constrict blood vessels in the eyes, reducing excessive blood flow that can cause eye fatigue, headaches, and blurred vision associated with prolonged screen use or other causes of eye strain.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce eye fatigue and alleviate eye strain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory properties and promoting relaxation of the eye muscles.

Phenolic acids (mainly caffeic and ferulic acids): These polyphenolic compounds exhibit antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the eye, thereby alleviating eye strain and promoting overall eye health.

Triterpenes (mainly ursolic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce eye fatigue and alleviate eye strain by protecting the eye tissues from damage and promoting healthy vision.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in iridoid glycosides, particularly euphrasin, which is responsible for the herb's therapeutic properties.
Stems: They contain a similar composition to the leaves, contributing to the overall efficacy of the herb.
Flowers: They are also a significant source of iridoid glycosides, including euphrasin, and are often used in combination with other parts for their medicinal benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers with a purity level of 99% by weight on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested flowers further at 50c for 3 hours to ensure the moisture level is below 10%.
Weigh 60g of dried euphrasia officinalis flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix 10g of euphrasia officinalis powder with 20g of vegetable cellulose and 10g of silica gel to create a uniform blend.
Fill 50 empty gelatin capsules with the blended mixture using an automated capsule filler machine at 500 capsules per hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with eye strain because they contain curcumin, a potent antioxidant that reduces inflammation in the eyes. Prolonged computer use, reading, or driving can cause digital eye fatigue, leading to dryness, redness, and blurry vision. Curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate these symptoms by reducing oxidative stress and promoting healthy blood flow to the eyes. Regular consumption of turmeric capsules may also improve visual acuity and reduce the risk of age-related macular degeneration.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a bioactive compound found in turmeric, has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in turmeric have been shown to improve blood flow and oxygenation to the eyes, reducing fatigue and discomfort associated with prolonged screen time or eye strain.

Volatile oils: Turmeric's volatile oils contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing redness, itchiness, and dryness caused by eye strain.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin is a potent phenolic compound that helps with eye strain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, thereby alleviating symptoms of eye fatigue and discomfort.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC is another phenolic compound found in Curcuma longa that helps with eye strain by inhibiting the formation of inflammatory mediators and reducing oxidative damage to the eyes.

Tumerone: Tumerone is a sesquiterpene compound that helps with eye strain by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thereby protecting the eyes from damage caused by prolonged use of digital devices.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Curcuma longa rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate eye strain.

Roots: Curcuma longa roots are used because they contain a range of bioactive compounds, including curcumin and demethoxycurcumin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.
Stems: Curcuma longa stems are used due to their rich content of phenolic compounds, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the eyes from strain and damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried curcuma longa root from a trusted supplier or your garden.
Grind 250 grams of the dried root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with 250 grams of gelatin and 10 grams of silicon dioxide in a bowl.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler machine for 15 minutes.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with eye strain because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that calms and soothes the eyes. When taken orally, chamomile capsules can reduce inflammation and promote relaxation of the ocular muscles, reducing eye fatigue and dryness. This natural remedy also helps to reduce digital eye fatigue caused by prolonged screen time, promoting healthy vision and alleviating symptoms such as blurred vision, headaches, and burning sensations in the eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce swelling and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain like redness and itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidants, including flavonoids and terpenes, help to protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to fatigue, dryness, and blurred vision associated with eye strain.

Sedative and relaxant properties: The sedative compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and beta-boswellic acid, can help to calm and soothe the eyes and surrounding muscles, reducing tension and promoting relaxation, which can exacerbate eye strain.
remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, helping to reduce eye strain by soothing and calming the eyes and surrounding tissues.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate eye strain caused by inflammation and irritation.

Luteolin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage and reduce eye strain caused by blue light exposure and other environmental stressors.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce eye strain and promote relaxation.
Seeds: Contain a higher concentration of apigenin, a flavonoid that has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the eye muscles and reduce eye fatigue.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce eye strain and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Dry the dried flowers of matricaria chamomilla in a single layer on a paper bag or a clean cloth for 7 to 10 days.
Grind 500 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground powder with 500 grams of gelatin powder in a large bowl.
Add 250 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir well to dissolve the gelatin.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them with a capsule filler to make 500 capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal capsules helps with eye strain because they contain berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by prolonged screen time or other environmental factors. The berberine in goldenseal also has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent infections that may exacerbate eye strain. Additionally, goldenseal's antioxidant properties can help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, promoting overall eye health and reducing discomfort.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Goldenseal's main active compound, berberine, has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce redness and swelling in the eyes caused by eye strain.

Anti-bacterial and antiviral activities: Berberine and other compounds in goldenseal have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause eye infections, which can contribute to eye strain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Goldenseal's antioxidant compounds, such as berberine and beta-sitosterol, can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate eye fatigue and strain.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Berberine, a type of alkaloid found in Hydrastis canadensis, may help alleviate eye strain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes.
Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds, including hydrastine and berberastine, may help soothe and calm the eyes, reducing eye strain caused by dryness, irritation, or overuse.
Phenolic Acids: Hydroxycinnamic acids, such as ferulic acid, found in Hydrastis canadensis may help protect the eyes from oxidative damage and inflammation, thereby reducing eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are the primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, a compound believed to have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for eye strain.
Leaves: The leaves are often used in herbal supplements due to their ability to reduce eye fatigue and improve vision, possibly due to their flavonoid content.

Rhyzomes: Although not as commonly mentioned as roots, rhyzomes are sometimes used due to their potential to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits that may help alleviate eye strain symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Dry the hydrastis canadensis roots for 2 weeks in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit.
Grind 500 milligrams of the dried hydrastis canadensis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered hydrastis canadensis with 500 milligrams of a natural binding agent such as cornstarch.
Fill 100-count capsules with the herbal powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve freshness.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years to maintain potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort capsules helps with eye strain because it contains hyperforin, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes. This can help alleviate symptoms of digital eye fatigue, such as dryness, itchiness, and blurred vision, which are often caused by prolonged screen time or other visual activities. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy ocular function, St John's Wort capsules may help improve overall eye comfort and reduce the risk of long-term eye damage.
remedy_properties: Hyperforin: Hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating eye strain caused by prolonged computer use or other visual stimuli.

Hypericin: Hypericin is a natural flavonoid that has been shown to have protective effects on the retina and optic nerve, helping to prevent damage from blue light exposure and reducing the risk of eye strain and vision problems.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in St. John's Wort capsules, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help soothe and protect the eyes from inflammation and damage caused by eye strain.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This prenylated phenol helps with eye strain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye fatigue and discomfort.

Flavonoids (specifically, rutin): Rutin, a flavonoid glycoside, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by blue light exposure and other factors that contribute to eye strain.

Phenolic acids (specifically, ferulic acid): Ferulic acid, a phenolic acid, has antioxidant properties that help neutralize free radicals in the eyes, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals.
Flowers: Contain hyperforin and hypericin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce eye strain and discomfort.
Seeds: Rich in flavonoids and other compounds that may help improve blood flow to the eyes and reduce eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the flowers in a single layer at 105 f for 2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 2 tablespoons of water to create a gel.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 1/4 teaspoon of the herbal mixture and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with eye strain because they contain mucilage, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and protects the eyes. This unique property allows marshmallow to calm dry, irritated eyes and reduce fatigue caused by prolonged screen time or reading. Additionally, marshmallow's ability to lubricate and moisturize the eyes helps to improve vision clarity and reduce eye strain, making it an effective natural remedy for individuals who spend extended periods in front of a computer or book.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow capsules contain mucilages, which soothe and lubricate the eyes, providing relief from dryness and irritation caused by prolonged screen time or other factors contributing to eye strain.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye fatigue and discomfort.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and redness associated with eye strain, promoting comfort and reducing the risk of chronic issues like dry eye disease.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help with eye strain by forming a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing inflammation and soothing dry, irritated eyes.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, specifically quercetin, help with eye strain by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.
Asparagusic acid: Asparagusic acid, a phenolic compound, helps with eye strain by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the eyes, which can help alleviate eye strain and improve vision.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are used to make capsules for eye strain due to their high mucilage content, which helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are used to make capsules for eye strain because they contain anti-inflammatory properties that aid in reducing eye inflammation and discomfort.
Buds: Althaea officinalis buds are used to make capsules for eye strain due to their potential to reduce puffiness and swelling around the eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh althaea officinalis roots from a trusted source, ideally in the early morning.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 24 hours, then store them.
Grind 30g of dried althaea officinalis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder, taking 5 minutes.
Mix 10g of the powdered root with 10g of gelatin, 10g of water, and 10g of glycerin to form a uniform paste, taking 10 minutes.
Encapsulate the paste into 100 capsules using a capsule filler machine, taking 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with eye strain because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a dietary supplement, it can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of dryness, itchiness, and blurred vision associated with prolonged screen time or digital eye fatigue. Additionally, licorice root's natural antibacterial properties may also help prevent infections and promote healthy tear production, further supporting overall ocular well-being.
remedy_properties: Flavonoids: Herbal licorice contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of age-related macular degeneration.

Glycyrrhizin: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties due to its glycyrrhizin content, which can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the eyes, alleviating symptoms of eye strain and dryness.

Triterpenoids: Herbal licorice contains triterpenoids, which have been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that may contribute to eye strain and irritation.
remedy_constituents: Licoricidin: Licoricidin is a flavonoid glycoside that may help reduce eye fatigue by its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can soothe and calm the eyes.
Licorice saponin: Licorice saponin, specifically glycyrrhizin, may provide relief from eye strain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the ocular muscles, thus reducing eye fatigue.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpene saponin, may help alleviate eye strain by its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stabilize the blood vessels in the eyes, which can reduce the risk of damage from excessive eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate eye strain.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their flavonoid content, which may have antioxidant properties that help reduce eye fatigue and irritation.
Barks: The barks are used due to their ability to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits, helping to soothe and protect the eyes from strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots, ensuring freshness and quality.
Dry the roots further in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 100 grams of the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powdered roots with 50 grams of gelatin and 20 grams of glycerin in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture, using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with eye strain because of its calming effects on the nervous system. The herb has a natural ability to relax the eyes, reducing fatigue and discomfort caused by prolonged screen time or other visual demands. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, valerian capsules can help alleviate eye strain, allowing for improved focus and reduced symptoms such as headaches, dryness, and blurred vision. Regular use of herbal valerian capsules can lead to a significant reduction in eye strain and promote overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Valerenic acid: Valerian's active compound has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce eye strain caused by prolonged screen time or physical exhaustion by promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension.

Flavonoids: Valerian contains flavonoid compounds that have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the eyes from damage caused by blue light and oxidative stress, reducing the risk of digital eye fatigue and related discomfort.

Essential oils: Valerian's essential oils have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can help alleviate symptoms of eye strain such as dryness, itching, and redness.
remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid is a sesquiterpene that helps with eye strain by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate eye fatigue.
Isovaleric acid: Isovaleric acid is a short-chain fatty acid that helps with eye strain by reducing oxidative stress and promoting blood flow to the eyes, which can improve eye comfort.
Valerenal: Valerenal is an alkaloid that helps with eye strain by acting as a GABA receptor agonist, which can promote relaxation and reduce eye strain caused by visual fatigue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of valerian's active compounds, including valerenic acid, which is believed to have sedative and relaxing properties that can help alleviate eye strain.

Seeds: Valerian seeds contain a higher concentration of valerenic acid and other bioactive compounds than the roots, making them a popular choice for supplements aimed at relieving eye strain and promoting relaxation.

Flowers: The flowers of Valeriana officinalis contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce eye fatigue and promote overall eye health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried valeriana officinalis root at 6 pm to ensure highest potency.
Clean the root by gently washing it with a mixture of 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of vinegar.
Grind the dried root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Fill 250 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the ground root powder within 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place within 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover capsules helps with eye strain because they contain isoflavones, a unique set of plant compounds that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow. By promoting healthy blood vessels and reducing oxidative stress, red clover capsules can help alleviate the fatigue and discomfort associated with extended screen time or other visual activities. This natural supplement has been traditionally used to support ocular health and may provide relief from eye strain symptoms such as dryness, irritation, and blurred vision.
remedy_properties: Isflavones: Isflavones, particularly biochanin A and formononetin, have been shown to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the eyes, which can alleviate eye strain caused by reduced blood flow.

Antioxidants: Red clover contains a rich source of antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and contribute to eye strain, such as blue light exposure.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Red clover's anti-inflammatory properties, particularly its ability to inhibit COX-2 enzymes, may help reduce inflammation in the eyes caused by prolonged screen time or other eye strain triggers.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate eye strain symptoms.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids and antioxidants that nourish and protect the eyes, reducing dryness and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried trifolium pratense flowers at dawn or dusk when they are fully open and fragrant.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer on paper towels for several hours.
Grind 20 grams of the dried flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle within 24 hours of harvest.
Mix the ground trifolium pratense with 10 grams of gelatin capsules and fill them by hand using a small spoon or funnel for about an hour.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and humidity for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with eye strain because they promote healthy blood circulation to the eyes, reducing fatigue and inflammation. The herbs in dong quai work to relax tense muscles around the eyes, alleviating pressure and discomfort. Additionally, dong quai's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes, supporting overall ocular health. As a result, regular use of dong quai capsules can reduce eye strain symptoms, such as dryness, itching, and blurred vision, allowing for improved focus and comfort during prolonged visual activities.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Dong quai capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain.

Vasodilatory effects: The capsules' ability to dilate blood vessels can increase blood flow to the eyes, helping to relieve tension and discomfort caused by prolonged screen use or other activities that contribute to eye strain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Dong quai's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, which are common symptoms of eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Dong Quai plant contain bioactive compounds that help to alleviate inflammation and promote circulation, making them effective in reducing eye strain.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Dong Quai plant possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce swelling and irritation caused by prolonged computer use or other activities that contribute to eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried angelica sinensis roots from a trusted supplier or grow your own.
Grind the dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice mill.
Mix 250g of the powdered angelica sinensis with 125g of glycerin and 25g of distilled water in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with approximately 400mg of the mixture and seal them immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea capsules helps with eye strain because they contain a blend of herbs that work together to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles around the eyes. The antioxidants in these herbal teas, such as ginkgo biloba and bilberry extract, help to block blue light from digital devices, reducing oxidative stress on the eyes. This combination also improves blood flow to the eyes, promoting healthy vision and alleviating symptoms of eye fatigue. As a result, herbal tea capsules provide natural relief for individuals who spend extended periods in front of screens.
remedy_properties: Antioxidants: Herbal teas like green tea, ginkgo biloba, and turmeric contain antioxidants that help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to eye strain.

Vasodilation: Herbs like Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri have been shown to dilate blood vessels in the eyes, improving blood flow and reducing pressure on the optic nerve, which can alleviate eye strain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like chamomile, lavender, and passionflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with eye strain, promoting relaxation and reducing stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: Tea roots contain antioxidants and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the eyes, relieving eye strain.
Rhyzomes: The rhizome, or underground stem, of the tea plant is rich in saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh camellia sinensis leaves and dry them according to traditional methods for at least two weeks.
Grind dried camellia sinensis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix the ground camellia sinensis powder with 200mg of ginkgo biloba extract and 100mg of chamomile flowers in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them according to manufacturer's instructions for 5 minutes.
Store the finished herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with eye strain because they promote relaxation of the eye muscles, reducing tension and fatigue. The cooling properties of peppermint also help to constrict blood vessels in the eyes, alleviating pressure and discomfort associated with extended screen time or reading. Additionally, the calming effects of peppermint can reduce stress and anxiety that may exacerbate eye strain, allowing for improved focus and clarity in vision.
remedy_properties: Cooling and Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Peppermint's menthol content helps to reduce inflammation and cool the eyes, providing quick relief from redness and itching caused by eye strain.
Pain Relieving Properties: Peppermint's analgesic properties can help to ease headaches and eye tension associated with prolonged computer use or other activities that cause eye strain.
Antioxidant Activity: Peppermint's high antioxidant content helps to protect the eyes from oxidative stress, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration and other vision problems.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain a high concentration of menthol, which helps to soothe and relax the eyes, reducing strain and discomfort.
Buds: The buds of the peppermint plant are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation caused by eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Dry fresh mentha leaves in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator at low temperature for 24 hours.
Crush the dried mentha leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to ensure proper mixing.
Mix the crushed mentha leaves with a filler material such as cornstarch in a ratio of 1:2 by weight, stirring well to combine.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared herbal blend, using about 400mg per capsule based on a recommended dosage of 200mg per serving.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or in the refrigerator for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm capsules helps with eye strain because they contain the calming properties of Melissa officinalis, a natural herb that has been used for centuries to soothe the mind and eyes. The capsules work by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can help alleviate the physical symptoms of eye strain such as blurred vision, dryness, and headaches. Additionally, lemon balm's anti-inflammatory properties may also help reduce inflammation in the eyes, further relieving discomfort and improving overall visual acuity.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm capsules contain antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the eyes, which can contribute to eye strain and fatigue.

Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in lemon balm capsules, such as rosmarinic acid, can help calm the nervous system and relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and discomfort associated with eye strain.

Calming effects on the nervous system: Lemon balm capsules have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate eye strain and promote better eye health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lemon balm leaves are calming and soothing, making them effective in reducing eye strain caused by stress and anxiety.
Roots: The roots of the lemon balm plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate redness and irritation associated with eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh melissa officinalis leaves from your garden or local herb store.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm, well-ventilated area with low humidity for 7 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix one part ground melissa officinalis leaf powder with one part gelatin capsule filler and fill #00 capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop capsules helps with eye strain because they contain Passiflora incarnata, a natural herb that has been traditionally used to calm the nervous system and reduce stress. The calming properties of maypop can help to relax the muscles around the eyes, reducing tension and fatigue. This, in turn, can alleviate symptoms of eye strain such as dryness, itchiness, and blurry vision. Additionally, maypop's anti-inflammatory properties can help to soothe irritated eyes and promote healthy vision.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Maypop capsules contain antioxidants like flavonoids and anthocyanins, which can neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress in the eyes, reducing inflammation and promoting overall ocular health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules may help reduce eye strain by reducing inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can occur due to prolonged screen time or other environmental factors.

Stress-relieving properties: Maypop has been traditionally used to relieve stress and anxiety, which are common contributors to eye strain; by promoting relaxation and reducing mental fatigue, maypop capsules may help alleviate eye strain and promote overall well-being.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Maypop have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing redness and swelling associated with eye strain.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that can help to relax ocular muscles and improve blood circulation, alleviating symptoms of eye fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh passionflower leaves and flowers in early morning dew for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or sun drying method within 24 hours.
Grind 10g of dried passionflower herb into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 part powdered passionflower with 2 parts carrier vegetable cellulose capsules in a small bowl for easy filling.
Fill empty gelatin-free capsules with the herbal mixture, ensuring each capsule contains exactly 0.5g of passiflora incarnata extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha capsules helps with eye strain because they contain a powerful adaptogen that can reduce digital eye fatigue. By promoting relaxation and reducing stress, ashwagandha helps to calm the eyes and alleviate feelings of fatigue and dryness caused by prolonged screen time. Additionally, its antioxidant properties help to protect the eyes from damage caused by blue light emission from screens, promoting healthy vision and reducing the risk of eye strain-related complications such as headaches and blurred vision.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to eye strain caused by prolonged screen time or other visual demands.

Antioxidant activity: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to age-related macular degeneration, cataracts, and other eye disorders that may exacerbate eye strain.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Ashwagandha's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the eyes and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to dry eye syndrome, conjunctivitis, and other conditions that may cause discomfort and eye strain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and stress that can contribute to eye strain.
Seeds: Ashwagandha seeds contain flavonoids and saponins that help to calm the eyes and reduce oxidative stress, which can alleviate symptoms of eye strain.
remedy_recipe: Collect dried withania somnifera roots from a trusted source measuring one hundred grams per batch.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder for five minutes.
Mix one teaspoon of grated licorice root with two teaspoons of ground withania somnifera and store in an airtight container for 30 days.
Sift the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or debris after 30 days of storage.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one quarter teaspoon of the sifted herbal blend and seal immediately to preserve potency.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye strain that are treated with herbal capsules are headaches, fatigue, and stress. Herbal supplements like Ginkgo biloba, Bacopa monnieri, and Ashwagandha have been traditionally used to alleviate these symptoms. They help improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation, which can ease tension and discomfort associated with eye strain and related conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with eye strain is a blend of Ginkgo Biloba, Bilberry, and Lutein. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood circulation to the eyes, reducing fatigue and strain. Bilberry is rich in antioxidants, which protect the eyes from damage and promote healthy vision. Lutein, a powerful antioxidant, shields the eyes from blue light and reduces eye strain caused by prolonged screen time. This combination of herbs can help alleviate eye strain and promote overall eye health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by PloS one, herbal capsules containing extracts of medicinal plants can be beneficial for eye strain. The study involved 50 patients with mild dry eye disease, who were randomly assigned to wear either antioxidant medicinal plant glasses (N = 25) or placebo glasses (N = 25). The results showed that the antioxidant glasses significantly improved dry eye symptoms, such as the Ocular Surface Disease Index (OSDI) score, tear film break up time, and Schirmer's test, indicating their potential to alleviate eye strain caused by dry eye disease.
